topic: Someone finds out their best friend is moving across the country
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope, thick and cream-colored, felt heavy in Maya’s hands. She traced the embossed lettering with a fingertip, her breath catching in her throat. "It's official," she whispered to the empty apartment, a wide, almost giddy smile stretching across her face. Liam was relocating. Not just a weekend trip, but a whole new state. Her heart hammered a frantic rhythm against her ribs, a joyful, skipping beat. She bounced on the balls of her feet, pacing the length of the living room, imagining the new city, the new adventures waiting just around the bend. She couldn't stop grinning; the sheer prospect of seeing him again, even across the miles, made her feel buoyant, like she was floating on air.

[story 2]

"You're serious?" Alex managed, his voice coming out a little too high-pitched. He reread the text message three times, the words about the cross-country move blurring slightly as a wave of pure elation washed over him. A slow, involuntary laugh bubbled up, shaking his shoulders. He threw his head back and let out a loud, unrestrained whoop, the sound echoing in the quiet room. He started doing little celebratory hops, his energy surging. This was it—the start of something huge. He felt a dizzying lightness in his chest, a powerful urge to just run, to grab his bags and chase the horizon. He couldn't contain the sheer thrill buzzing under his skin.

[story 3]

When Sarah finally opened the letter, the paper seemed to shimmer under the afternoon light. Her eyes scanned the details, and a warmth spread through her core, chasing away any lingering doubt. A wide, uninhibited smile bloomed on her face, reaching her eyes. "He's actually doing it," she murmured, a sound full of disbelief and sheer delight. She immediately sprang up from the sofa, clapping her hands together sharply. Her movements were quick, almost jerky with anticipation. She started humming a ridiculously upbeat tune, her whole body vibrating with an almost electric sense of forward momentum. The world suddenly seemed brighter, charged with possibility.
